_comment: Human-labelled ground truth for evaluation. Each entity has an 'id', 'type', and 'description'. Ambiguous cases are flagged with 'ambiguous': true — these are expected to have lower inter-rater agreement and the engine should mark low confidence.

decisions.description: Set pricing at $85/seat/month with annual plan at $75/seat
Use per-seat pricing model instead of flat fee
Review pricing again after 10 paying customers
Pass on VP Sales candidate and hire an AE instead
Hire AE once 12 customers are signed

reasons.description: One pilot flagged $75 as the budget-approval threshold; $85 stays above the veto while below $100 where two pilots said felt reasonable
Per-seat aligns revenue with customer growth; HR teams already track headcount making the metric intuitive
$180k base on $240k ARR with 6 months runway is existential risk; sales process not yet fully documented
12 customers provides a proven repeatable sales playbook and more runway comfort

evidence.description: Two pilots mentioned $100/seat feels reasonable; one said they'd need budget approval above $75
Company has $240k ARR, 6 months runway, 8 deals closed by CEO

goals.description: Build a repeatable sales process before hiring sales staff

constraints.description: $40k/month burn rate with 6 months runway — limits high fixed-cost hires
New paying customer onboarding next week — pricing decision needed before end of month

open_questions.description: What is the right time to raise prices?
When exactly should the AE hire happen relative to fundraising?

action_items.description: Close 2 more deals before hiring AE

decisions.description: Prioritise SSO integration for Q1 delivery by Dec 15
Hire a contractor for the reporting dashboard targeting Jan 10 delivery
Marcus to add query optimisation this week as temporary fix for database performance

reasons.description: Two enterprise prospects are blocking on SSO; estimated 3 weeks to deliver; unblocks $40k ARR
8 of 8 customers mentioned inability to show CFO ROI without better reporting; retention risk
Mobile app is 6-10 weeks of work; SSO and reporting are higher urgency
Maintenance window not viable during SSO sprint with enterprise prospects watching; migration can wait until February

evidence.description: Customer interview showed all 8 current customers cannot show ROI to their CFO without better reporting
Database query times creeping up; largest customer at 8k records; performance issues expected at 50k records; ~4 months at current growth rate

goals.description: Close two enterprise prospects currently blocked on SSO
Reduce churn risk by delivering reporting dashboard

constraints.description: SSO delivery deadline Dec 15 — enterprise prospects are watching
Team has no experience working with contractors — ramp time risk
P0 API rate limit issue causing intermittent failures must be fixed regardless of roadmap

open_questions.description: What is the fallback plan if the reporting contractor doesn't work out?
Will the database query optimisation be sufficient to hold until February?

action_items.description: Marcus delivers SSO integration by Dec 15
Priya hires reporting contractor this week
Sarah updates two SSO prospects today
Marcus fixes API rate limit issue this week
Marcus adds query optimisation this week

decisions.description: Stop looking for a co-founder; build the team through hires instead
No PLG model — product requires hands-on onboarding
No enterprise deals this year; focus on SMB
No product pivot; stay with workforce planning
First hire is a senior engineer in January

reasons.description: What is needed is someone to own sales (VP Sales role), not a technical co-founder; the search has been going on 4 months without result
Three competitors tried PLG and are struggling; product requires onboarding and hand-holding in month one
Enterprise sales cycles are 9-12 months; security audit and BAA required; no time with current runway
Have paying customers and a working product; pivoting now would kill morale and burn runway

evidence.description: Three named competitors attempted PLG and are struggling
Enterprise deal would require security audit, BAA; 9-12 month sales cycles; Fortune 500 inbound identified
Advisor Lisa said: 'You're not looking for a co-founder, you're looking for an excuse not to make a decision'

goals.description: Reach $50k MRR to raise Series A from a position of strength
Close Series A or bridge round before June runway end

constraints.description: Runway runs out in June — Series A or bridge conversations must start by December
Current MRR $18k — below typical Series A floor of $50-80k MRR
No enterprise deals this year due to time, compliance, and sales cycle constraints

open_questions.description: Bridge round vs Series A — which fundraising path to take?
When exactly should fundraise outreach begin?
Should the senior engineer be hired before or after starting fundraise?

action_items.description: Start Series A or bridge outreach by December (6 weeks away)

decisions.description: Did not charge customers for the first 12 months (retrospective — described as a mistake)
Pivoted roadmap to enterprise features in month 8 after first enterprise pilot signed
Tolerated non-shipping CTO from month 14 to month 22 without resolution
Implicitly decided to keep founding team intact despite performance issues — ambiguous whether this was a conscious decision or drift

reasons.description: Advisor said 'get to 50 customers before thinking about pricing'
One signed $60k ARR enterprise contract and two enterprise inbounds
Replacing a co-founder mid-company is terrifying — acknowledged constraint that was not acted on

evidence.description: Two customers told them unsolicited they would pay immediately; one offered $500/month — counter-evidence that was ignored
Existing 40 SMB users were churning at 8%/month — counter-evidence that was ignored
Enterprise contracts never closed; 2000-person company built internal tool instead; two inbounds ghosted after 4 months

goals.description: Reach 50 customers (original goal from advisor advice)
Build sustainable B2B SaaS business for customer success teams

constraints.description: Author recommends: no enterprise deal worth less than $100k ARR, and only after 20 retained SMB customers
NPS of 72 among active users — product worked; failure was strategic not product-market fit

open_questions.description: What would have happened if co-founder performance had been addressed in month 15?
Would charging from day one have changed the customer cohort quality?

action_items.description: Author recommends: enforce written decision + counter-argument for every major decision
Author recommends: define enterprise readiness as a deliberate milestone with explicit criteria
